quoteLast: , the galleon hauled up her fore-sail, and brought to under top-sails, with her head to the northward, hoisting Spanish colours, and having the standard of Spain flying at the top-gallant mast-head.
title: An historical account of all the voyages round the world, performed by English navigators, vol. 2 of 4
author: Henry, David, 1710-1792 [Editor]

quoteLast: The French fleet was badly beaten, and Villaret-Joyeuse at the end of the day drew back to Brest only a battered, splintered and ragged remnant of the fine squadron which he had commanded.
title: The Life of Captain Matthew Flinders
author: Scott, Ernest, Sir, 1868-1939

quoteLast: General Burnside and staff passed by.
title: Diary of Battery A, First Regiment Rhode Island Light Artillery
author: Reichardt, Theodore

quoteFirst: If the morning sortie has been an early one, we go up again
quoteTime: about one o'clock in the afternoon
title: Flying for France: With the American Escadrille at Verdun
author: McConnell, James R. (James Rogers), 1887-1917; Rockwell, Paul Ayres, 1889- [Photographer]

quoteLast: when the Scharnhorst and the Gneisenau turned to port to engage the Invincible and the Inflexible, the enemy's light cruisers turned to starboard to escape.
title: History of the World War
author: March, Francis A., Jr., 1863-1928; Beamish, Richard J. (Richard Joseph), 1867-1945

quoteLast: we again started, and proceeded about four miles in the direction of E.S.E. We now reached a pretty little stream, whose limpid waters flow over a bed of granite: its course is from south to N.N.E. The natives call it Naufomou, and they informed me that it emptied itself into the Rio Nuñez.
title: Travels through Central Africa to Timbuctoo; and across the Great Desert, to Morocco, performed in the years 1824-1828, Vol. 1 (of 2)
author: Caillié, René, 1799-1838

quoteFirst: When we reached the second ford,
quoteLast: , we found that the bank was not yet made passable for the wagons and artillery, so we drew up along the shingle until this could be done.
title: A Soldier of Virginia: A Tale of Colonel Washington and Braddock's Defeat
author: Stevenson, Burton Egbert, 1872-1962

quoteLast: , the twenty-five guns at Posthenen, given the order by the Emperor, fired a volley, and battle was joined all along the line.
title: The Memoirs of General Baron de Marbot
author: Marbot, Jean-Baptiste-Antoine-Marcelin, baron de, 1782-1854; Colt, Oliver C. [Translator]

quoteLast: a shout was heard from Stanley: “Now, men, look out! prepare for a sight of the Nyanza!”
title: Stanley's Emin Pasha expedition
author: Wauters, A.-J. (Alphonse-Jules), 1845-1916
